title: Multi-runtime agent support: Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI
description: Abstract pi-specific coupling so pi-messenger's messaging mesh and Crew orchestration work with Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini CLI. Requires extracting a runtime-agnostic core, building spawn adapters per CLI, MCP tool surface for non-pi agents, and progress parser adapters.
close_reason: Implemented: PR #9 with RuntimeAdapter, pi-messenger-cli, ClaudeAdapter, completion inference. 43/47 tasks, 394 tests. 4 items deferred to follow-up.

title: Context-aware poll timeout — fix D5 spawn regression
close_reason: Completed — spawnPollTimeoutMs config (900s default), resolveSpawnPollTimeout helper

title: Remove fixed timeouts from collaborator blocking exchange
description: Spec 004 blocking exchange added hardcoded 600s/300s timeouts that contradict the deterministic design principle. Collaborators that are actively working (log growing) get killed by the wall clock. The three deterministic exit conditions (message received, crash detected, user cancelled) are sufficient. Remove fixed timeouts, replace with progress-aware stall detection.
close_reason: Completed — spec 005 implemented, 448 tests passing, pushed to fork

title: Harden PR #9 for upstream submission
description: PR #9 (spec 002 multi-runtime support) has 8 findings from adversarial review that block upstream submission: personal env data leak in committed JSONL, PR too large (5600 lines with process artifacts), duplicated stuck detection logic, as-any type bypasses in CLI, completion inference false-positive risk in multi-worker mode, nonce auth oversold as security (is defense-in-depth only), 23 unsquashed commits with bookkeeping noise, and CodexAdapter built from insufficient samples. Must systematically address all findings to produce a clean, reviewable, high-quality contribution.
close_reason: All 8 findings addressed, PR #9 hardened and force-pushed with 6 bisectable commits, 421 tests passing

title: pi-messenger-cli not installed globally — unreachable from non-pi runtimes
close_reason: Completed — CLI globally available with spawn/dismiss, jiti wrapper, Homebrew symlink

title: Blocking collaborator exchange — eliminate ambiguous waiting states
description: Make spawn block until collaborator's first message and send block until reply for collaborator exchanges. Eliminates the LLM forward-bias problem where spawning agents ping early, dismiss, or go solo during the 3-10 minute gap after spawn returns. Shape A selected: inbox polling with watcher filter. See specs/004-blocking-collab-exchange/shaping.md for full requirements (R0-R9) and fit check.

title: Structured phase protocol — deterministic collaboration termination
description: Add structured phase field to send action and auto-terminate conversations when both sides signal completion. Eliminates the last-message stall where the driver blocks indefinitely after sending a final acknowledgment to a collaborator that has already finished. Three enforcement layers: D2 (driver phase:complete → non-blocking), D4 (peer phase:complete → auto-terminate next send), D5 (message-based stall detection independent of log growth).
close_reason: Implemented: structured phase protocol with D2/D4/D5 layered enforcement. 8 commits, 443 tests, 12 new.
